Est. early 1990s

Our History

Three decades of folding, friendship, and the pursuit of an ancient art.

The Beginning

Where It All Started

The Greater San Diego Origami Group was founded in the early 1990s by two remarkable women whose passion for paper folding sparked a community that has flourished ever since.

Florence Temko was a prolific author who wrote dozens of books on origami and paper crafts, helping introduce the art to countless beginners. Her gift was making origami feel welcoming, joyful, and accessible to anyone willing to pick up a square of paper.

V'Ann Cornelius brought an artist's eye and a curator's vision. She co-curated Origami Masterworks, one of the first major museum-based origami exhibitions in the world, at Mingei International Museum in Balboa Park. Her innovations in displaying origami as fine art helped elevate the medium.

Together, they created a space where folders of all levels could gather, learn, and be inspired. That spirit endures today.

Read the Tribute
Mission

What Drives Us

To share the joy of paper folding, nurture its growth, bring people together, and encourage community among paper folders.

We welcome absolute beginners and experienced artists alike. What unites us isn't skill level — it's a shared wonder at what a single sheet of paper can become.

Milestones

A Timeline of Folding

Early 1990s

The Group Is Founded

Florence Temko and V'Ann Cornelius establish the Greater San Diego Origami Group.

Mid 1990s

Origami Masterworks Exhibition

V'Ann Cornelius co-curates Origami Masterworks at Mingei International Museum in Balboa Park — one of the first major museum-based origami exhibitions in the world.

2000s

Growing Membership

The group grows through monthly meetings, workshops, and community events, welcoming folders of all ages and skill levels.

Add Your Date

Add a Milestone

Edit this file to add important moments. Each timeline-item block is a new event — just copy and paste.

Present

Arnold Tubis — Co-Coordinator

Arnold Tubis, retired physics professor and internationally exhibited origami designer, serves as co-coordinator. Author of nine origami books and over 50 articles, Arnold focused on using origami to enrich the teaching of geometry in schools. Visit his site →

GSDOG

Greater San Diego Origami Group
San Diego, California
